name: Canyon river Ubagua from Riezu
region_intro: Hiking around Valle De Yerri offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Sierra de Urbasa and Andía to the north, and traversed by the Irantzu and Ubagua Rivers. The region features impressive river canyons, such as the Ubagua River Canyon and Erbioz gorge, alongside extensive holm oak, beech, and oak woodlands. The terrain includes varied elevations, from river valleys to the higher protected areas, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Valle De Yerri The most popular hiking route is Canyon river Ubagua from Riezu, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Ubagua River, offering views of its cliffs. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Blas Hermitage – Source of the Ubagua River loop from 43. fazeria, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This trail explores the river's source and leads to a historic hermitage. Local hikers also love the Altikogaña – MiradorDeLazkua loop from Eraul, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking around Valle De Yerri is defined by river canyons, diverse forests, and protected natural areas like Sierra de Urbasa. The network offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and more challenging routes. The routes in Valle De Yerri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Yerri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valle De Yerri?
A: Valle De Yerri offers a diverse network of nearly 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 2 easy, 14 moderate, and 2 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valle De Yerri is an excellent destination for families and beginners. The region features a score of easy-to-do trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15001040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ubagua River – San Blas Hermitage loop from 43. fazeria&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6 km path, perfect for a relaxed outing. The Sierra de Urbasa and Andía area, bordering the valley, is also known for pleasant walks ideal for family excursions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Valle De Yerri?
A: Hiking in Valle De Yerri offers a rich variety of terrain. You'll encounter impressive river canyons, such as the Ubagua River Canyon and Erbioz gorge, alongside extensive holm oak, beech, and oak woodlands. The landscape ranges from river valleys to higher protected areas like the Sierra de Urbasa and Andía, providing varied elevations and scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Valle De Yerri?
A: Yes, many trails in Valle De Yerri are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15001335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Blas Hermitage – Source of the Ubagua River loop from 43. fazeria&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altikogaña – MiradorDeLazkua loop from Eraul&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Valle De Yerri is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970126219/canyon-river-ubagua-from-riezu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyon of the Ubagua River&lt;/a&gt;, known for its magnificent cliffs. The Erbioz gorge is another captivating landscape with diverse vegetation and is recognized for its ornithological interest, especially for griffon vultures. Don't miss the monumental oak tree, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de Erául&lt;/a&gt;, declared a National Monument.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The historic Monasterio de Iranzu is a significant cultural attraction in the region, often integrated into walking routes. Additionally, some trails may lead you past other notable sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7387615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/382711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valle De Yerri?
A: Valle De Yerri is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer can be warm but is still enjoyable, especially for routes along rivers or in forested areas. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher or more exposed trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Valle De Yerri?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and villages throughout Valle De Yerri. For specific routes like the Canyon of the Ubagua River from Riezu, you'll typically find designated parking areas in or near the starting village. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking recommendations for your chosen hike.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While Valle De Yerri is best explored by car for maximum flexibility, some villages and towns within or near the valley may have limited public transport connections. For detailed information on bus routes and schedules to specific trailheads, it's recommended to consult local transport services or the official tourism website for Navarre: &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/hiking" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Valle De Yerri offers more demanding routes. There are 2 difficult trails available, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38286500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area Under Olive Trees – Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa loop from Lorca / Lorka&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 25.9 km path. The region also features longer GR® trails (over 50 km) that traverse the area, providing extensive hiking opportunities.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Valle De Yerri?
A: The hiking experiences in Valle De Yerri are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.5 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, the well-maintained trails, and the stunning river canyons. Many appreciate the variety of routes suitable for all levels, from leisurely family walks to more challenging excursions.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting in Valle De Yerri?
A: Valle De Yerri's diverse ecosystems provide good opportunities for wildlife spotting. The Erbioz gorge, for example, is noted for its ornithological interest, particularly for griffon vultures. The Sierra de Urbasa and Andía area is home to freely grazing livestock, and the various forests and river habitats support a range of local fauna, making it a rewarding area for nature observation.
tips: A conditioned path runs through the entire canyon, it is worth seeing, although it would be more beautiful if the entire course carried water.
Signposted site on the itinerary of the Ubagua River Canyon indicating its source.
Starting the itinerary from Riezu, a few km away is this beautiful Hermitage, one of the places of interest on the Ubagua River Canyon.
